hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3444 - وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ر َّو َّح َّ إ َّمَّي َّهُّ مِن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ  وَّ س َّزَّطَّ لَّهُّ إ َّن  َّ مَّا 
وَّلَّذَّب ْ مِن ْ وَّلَّذٍ ق َّهُّوَّ جُّز   ق َّطَّل َّق َّهَّا ر َّوْج ُّهَّا إ َّوْ مَّاب َّ عَّن ْهَّا ق َّز َّو َّج َّهَّا مِن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ ج َّزَّ مَّا مَّن ْز ِلَّه ُّ وَّلَّ ذِهَّا ق َّال 
ذ َّلِك َّ لِلْا َّو َّلِ وَّ هُّوَّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ ج َّزِ ن ِالْح ِي َّارِ إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إ َّعْي َّق َّ وَّ  إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إ َّمْسَّك
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Sama'ah, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who said 
to three of his slaves, "You are free," while he actually had four slaves.  
Then someone said to him: "You have freed all your slaves." 
He replied: “Yes." 
I asked: “Is the emancipation binding for all four because he generalized them, or is it 
limited to the three whom he specifically mentioned?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Emancipation is binding only for those he specifically freed."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.115 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 p.226 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 
p.616 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.94 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3443

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from al -Halabi, from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, regarding a man who married his bondwoman to another 
man and stipulated that any child she bore would be free. Then her husband either 
divorced her or died, and she was married to another man.  
I asked: “What is the status of her child in this case?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "The child is regarded like her. That condition applied only to the first 
husband. As for the second husband, he has the option - if he wishes, he may free the 
child, and if he wishes, he may keep the child as a slave."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.116 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 p.225 • Al-Wafi, Vol.23 
p.1405 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.21 p.124 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3444

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3446  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ : عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّالَّ لِع ُّلاَّمِهِ إ ُّعْي ِق ُّك َّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ْ إ ُّر َّوِّج َّك ج َّارِي َّي ِى  هَّذ ِةِ ق َّا ِن ْ 
ن َّكَّحْث َّ عَّلَّي ْهَّا إ َّوْ ي َّسَّز َّي ْث َّ ق َّعَّلَّي ْك َّ مِان َّه ُّ ذِي ن َّارٍ ق َّا َّعْي َّق َّهُّ عَّلَّى  ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّن َّكَّحَّ إ َّوْ ي َّسَّز َّى إ َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ مِان َّه ُّ ذِي ن َّارٍ وَّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ س َّزْطُّهُّ 
ق َّال
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT}, peace be upon him and his family, said: 
"There is no divorce before marriage, and there is no emancipation before ownership." 
[REFERENCES] Qurb Al -Isnad, Vol. 1 p.104 • Al -Kafi, Vol. 6 p.179 • Da’a’im Al -Islam, Vol. 2 p.98 • Man La 
Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.116 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.217 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.5 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 
p.584 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.22 p.33 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.15 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.16 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3445

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.en:
Abd al -Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah asked him about a man who said to his 
slave, "I will free you on the condition that I marry you to this bondwoman of mine. 
However, if you marry another woman or take another concubine, you will be liable for 
one hundred dinars." 
Then he freed him based on this condition, and the slave either married another 
woman or took another concubine. 
Abd al-Rahman asked: “Is he liable for the one hundred dinars, and is his condition 
valid?" 
Imam {a.s} said: "His condition is valid, and he is liable for it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.116 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.595 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.23 
p.27 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3446

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.en:
Abu Abdullah {a.s}, was asked about a man who freed his slave on the condition 
that he marries his daughter and stipulated that if he marries another woman or takes 
another concubine, he would be liable for a specific amount. 
Imam {a.s} said: "It is permissible." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.116 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.595 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.23 
p.27 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3447

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3449  - وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ وَّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْي َّق َّ ع ن ْذإً لَّهُّ مَّال  لِمَّن ْ  
مَّالُّ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ ق َّال » 
وَّ ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ ن َّاعَّ مَّمْلُّوكاً وَّ لَّهُّ مَّال  ق َّال س ْي َّزِى  وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّعْلَّمِ إَّلْن َّان ِغُّ 
ق َّالْمَّالُّ لِلْن َّان ِغِ
hadiths.en:
Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked him about a man who freed his bondwoman on the 
condition that she serves him for five years. However, she ran away, and then the man 
died. 
Ya'qub asked: “If his heirs find her, do they have the right to make her serve them?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "No." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.117 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3448

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3450  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إِن ْن ُّ ن ُّكَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
ي َّعْلَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ لَّهُّ مَّالأً وَّ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ِ إِسْي َّي ْن َّى إَّلس َّي ِّذُّ إَّلْمَّالَّ جِي ن َّ إ َّعْي َّق َّهُّ ق َّهُّوَّ لِلْعَّن ْذِ
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Zurarah, from Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -
Baqir {a.s} and Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, regarding a man 
who freed a slave that had wealth. 
The Imams {a.s} said: "If he knew that the slave had wealth, then the wealth belongs to 
the freed slave. But if he did not know, then the wealth belongs to the one who 
emancipated him." 
And regarding a man who sold a slave that had wealth, the Imams {a.s} said: 
"If the seller knew that the slave had wealth, then the wealth belongs to the buyer. But 
if the seller did not know, then the wealth belongs to the seller."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.117 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3449

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3451   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّه عَّن ْذُّ إَّلز َّجْمَّن ِ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ : عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْي َّق َّ عَّن ْذإً لَّهُّ وَّ لِلْعَّن ْذِ مَّال  ق َّي ُّوُّق ِّى َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  إ َّ عْي َّق َّ إَّلْعَّن ْذَّ لِمَّن ْ 
ي َّكُّون ُّ مَّالُّ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ إ َّ ي َّكُّون ُّ لِل َّذ ِى  إ َّعْي َّق َّ إَّلْعَّن ْذَّ إ َّوْ لِلْعَّن ْذِ ق َّال وَّ هُّوَّ ي َّعْلَّمُّ إ َّن  َّ لَّهُّ مَّالأً ق َّمَّالُّهُّ لَّهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّعْلَّمْ 
ق َّمَّالُّهُّ لِوُّلْذِ سَّي ِّذِةِ
hadiths.en:
Ibn Bukayr narrated from Zurarah,  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
"If a man has a slave and frees him, knowing that the slave has wealth, and the master 
did not exclude the wealth at the time of freeing him, then the wealth belongs to the 
slave." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol. 6 p.190 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.117 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 
p.223 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.10 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.669 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.47 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3450

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3452  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّعْي َّق َّ مَّمْلُّوكَّهُّ عِن ْذَّ مَّوْ ي ِهِ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ذَّي ْن   
ق َّال إَّل َّذ ِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ مِي ْلَّهُّ ج َّار َّ عِي ْق ُّهُّ وَّ إ ِلأ َّ لَّمْ ي َّح ُّز ْ
hadiths.en:
Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Abdullah asked him about a man who freed his slave, 
and the slave had wealth. Then the man who freed the slave passed away.  
Abd al-Rahman asked: “To whom does the wealth of the slave belong? Does it go to 
the one who freed the slave, or does it belong to the slave?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "If he freed him knowing that the slave had wealth, then the wealth 
belongs to the slave. But if he did not know, then the wealth belongs to the children of 
his master." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.117 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.223 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.11 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.670 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.49 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3451
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٨، ح ٣٤٥١

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3453  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّم َّاذ  عَّن ِ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّق ُّولُّ إ ِن ْ مِث  ُّ ق َّعَّن ْذِى  جُّز   وَّ عَّلَّى إَّلز َّج ُّ لِ  
ذَّي ْن   ق َّال   ن ِ ي َّمَّن ِ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ إُّسْي ُّسْعِى َّ إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ  
ق ِى  ق َّص َّاءِ ذَّي ْن ِ مَّوْلأَّةُّ وَّ هُّوَّ جُّز   ن ِهِ إ ِذ َّإ إ َّوْق َّاة
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Zurarah,  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar Al -Sadiq {a.s}, 
regarding a man who freed his slave at the time of his death while he was in debt.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If the value of the slave equals the amount of his debt or more, the 
emancipation is valid. Otherwise, it is not valid." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.27 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.118 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, 
Vol.4 p.224 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.232 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.218 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 p.7 • Al-
Wafi, Vol.10 p.621 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.356 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3452
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٨، ح ٣٤٥٢

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.en:
Hammad narrated from al -Halabi, from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}, that he was asked about a man who said:  
“If I die, my slave is free," while the man was in debt. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If he dies and his debt equals the value of the slave, the slave shall be 
sold to pay off the debt. But if the debt does not equal value of the slave, the slave 
must work to pay off his master's debt, and he becomes free once debt is fully repaid." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.119 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.232 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.9 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.620 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3453
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٨، ح ٣٤٥٣

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.ar:
3455  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّزِي ز   عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ي َّزَّك مَّمْلُّوكاً ن َّي ْن َّ  ن َّق َّزٍ 
ق َّس َّهِذَّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمْ إ َّن  َّ إَّلْمَّي ِّث َّ إ َّعْي َّق َّهُّ ق َّال  ق ِى  ن َّصِي ن ِهِ وَّ إُّسْي ُّسْعِى َّ  
إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ ق ِي مَّا كَّان َّ لِلْوَّرَّي َّه ِ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Marwan narrated from Imam {a.s} who said: 
"My father, peace be upon him, left sixty slaves and instructed in his will to free one -
third of them. So, I cast lots among them and selected twenty, then I freed them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.119 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 p.234 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 
p.615 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3454
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٨، ح ٣٤٥٤

hadiths.chapter: 48. CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
hadiths.en:
Hariz narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams {a.s}, 
peace be upon them, who said: 
I asked Imam {a.s} about a man who left a slave jointly owned by several people, and 
one of them testified that the deceased had freed the slave.  
Imam {a.s} said: "If the witness is trustworthy, he does not have to compensate the 
others, and his testimony is valid for his share. As for the remaining shares belonging 
to the heirs, the slave must work to pay off their value."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.119 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol.8 p.234 • Al-Wafi, Vol.25 
p.944 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.324 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.88

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 48, H. 3455
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON EMANCIPATION AND ITS RULINGS

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3457  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي ل  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّذَّن  َّزِ إ َّ ي ُّن َّاعُّ ق َّال  صَّاجِن ُّهُّ إ ِلَّى 
ي َّمَّن ِهِ وَّ رَّص ِى َّ إَّلْمَّمْلُّوك ُّ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْس
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim) {a.s}, 
about a man who frees his slave as a delayed emancipation (to take effect after his 
death) and then becomes in need of the slave’s price. 
Imam {a.s} said: "He may sell him." 
I asked: “What if he is not in need of the price?" 
Imam {a.s} said: "If the slave agrees, then there is no harm." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.120 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3456
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٥٦

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3458  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّعْي ِق ُّ ع ُّلاَّمَّهُّ إ َّوْ ج َّارِي َّي َّهُّ 
عَّن ْ ذُّن ُّزٍ مِن ْهُّ ي ُّم َّ ي َّحْي َّاح ُّ إ ِلَّى ي َّمَّن ِهِ إ َّ ي َّن ِي عُّهُّ ق َّال اةُّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّعْي ِق َّهُّ عِن ْذَّ مَّوْي ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Jamil narrated from Abu Abdullah Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}, who said: 
I asked him about a mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death).  
Imam {a.s} said: "If his owner needs the price and the slave agrees, then there is no harm 
in selling him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.120 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3457
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٥٧

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3459  -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو إ ِن ْزَّإهِي مَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إِمْزَّإ َّة ٍ ذَّن  َّزَّب ْ ج َّارِي َّه ً لَّهَّا ق َّوَّلَّذَّب ِ إَّلْح َّارِي َّه ُّ ج َّارِي َّه ً ن َّق ِي سَّه ً ق َّلَّمْ ي ُّذْرَّ إ َّ 
مُّذَّن  َّزَّة   هِى َّ مِي ْلُّ إ ُّمِّهَّا إ َّمْ لأَّ ق َّق َّال »
إ َّج ِن ْن ِى  ق ِي هِ مَّا ج َّمِي عاً ق َّق َّال ه ُّ مُّذَّن  َّزَّة   وَّ مَّا ق ِى   
ن َّطْن ِهَّا رِق    وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إَّلي  َّذْن ِي زُّ ق َّن ْلَّ إَّلْحَّمْلِ ي ُّم َّ جَّذَّب َّ إَّلْحَّمْلُّ ق َّالْوَّلَّذُّ مُّذَّن  َّ ز  مَّغَّ إ ُّمِّهِ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلْحَّمْلَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا جَّذَّب َّ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلي  َّذْن ِي زِ
hadiths.en:
It is narrated by al-Ala’, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams 
{a.s}, peace be upon them, regarding a man who frees his male or female slave as a 
mudabbar (to be freed after his death) and then becomes in need of the slave’s price.  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, he may not sell the slave  - unless he stipulates to the buyer that 
the slave must be freed at the time of his death." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.120 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.263 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.28 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.432 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.629 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.117 • Mustadrak Al-
Wasa’il, Vol.16 p.5 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3458
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٥٨

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3460  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إَّلْحَّسَّن ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّلِى ٍّ إَّلْوَّس  َّاءُّ إ َّن َّا إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ ذَّن  َّزَّ ج َّارِي َّه ً وَّ هِى جُّن ْلَّى ق َّق َّال 
عَّلِمَّ ن ِحَّن َّلِ إَّلْح َّارِي َّه ِ ق َّمَّا ق ِى  ن َّطْن ِهَّا ن ِمَّن ْز ِلَّي ِهَّا وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ لَّمْ ي َّعْلَّمْ ق َّمَّا ق ِ ى  ن َّطْن ِهَّا رِق 
إَّلْمَّمْلُّوك َّ وَّ هُّوَّ جَّسَّن ُّ إَّلْحَّالِ ي ُّم َّ ي َّحْي َّاح ُّ إ َّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ِي عَّهُّ ق َّال ك
hadiths.en:
Abu Ibrahim (Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s}) was asked about a woman 
who made her bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after her death). The bondwoman 
later gave birth to a precious daughter, and it was unclear whether the child was also 
mudabbar like her mother or not. 
Imam {a.s} said: "When the pregnancy occurred, was it before or after the tadbir 
(declaration of delayed emancipation)?" 
I said: “May I be your ransom, I do not know. Please answer regarding both situations." 
Imam {a.s} said: "If the bondwoman was pregnant before the tadbir and her pregnancy 
was not mentioned during the declaration, then the bondwoman is mudabbar, but 
what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar). 
However, if the tadbir occurred before the pregnancy and then she became pregnant, 
the child is mudabbar along with her, because the pregnancy occurred after the 
tadbir." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.120 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3459
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٥٩

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3461   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
ي َّزْج ِغَّ ق ِى  ي ُّلُّي ِهِ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ إ َّوْصَّى ق ِى  صِح َّه ٍ إ َّوْ مَّزَّض ٍ
hadiths.en:
Al-Hasan ibn Ali al-Washsha’ asked Abu al-Hasan {a.s} about a man who made 
his bondwoman mudabbar (to be freed after his death) while she was pregnant. 
Imam {a.s} said: "If he knew about the pregnancy of the bondwoman, then what is in her 
womb has the same status as her (i.e., it is also mudabbar). But if he did not know, 
then what is in her womb remains in bondage (not mudabbar)." 
Al-Hasan ibn Ali al -Washsha’ further asked him about a man who made his slave 
mudabbar while he was financially well-off, but later he became needy. 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, if he becomes in need, it is permissible for him to sell the slave."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.121 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3460
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٦٠

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3462  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  مَّزْي َّمَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سُّن ِلَّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّعْي ِق ُّ ج َّارِي َّي َّ هُّ عَّن ْ ذُّن ُّزٍ إ َّ  
ي َّطَّو ُّهَّا إ ِن ْ س َّاءَّ إ َّوْ ي ُّن ْكِحُّهَّا إ َّوْ ي َّن ِي غُّ ج ِذْمَّي َّهَّا جَّي َّاي َّهُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from al -Ala’, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the 
Imams {a.s}, peace be upon them, who said: 
"A mudabbar (a slave promised freedom after the owner's death) is considered to be 
from the one -third (of the estate). A man has the right to retract from his bequest 
regarding this one-third, whether he made the will during his health or illness."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.7 p.22 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.121 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.9 p.225 
• Al-Wafi, Vol.24 p.74 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.19 p.307 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.119 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3461
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٤٩، ح ٣٤٦١

hadiths.chapter: 49. CHAPTER ON CONDITIONAL EMANCIPATION
hadiths.ar:
3463  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّاصِم  عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْعَّن ْذِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّمَّه ِ ي ُّعْي َّق َّان ِ عَّن ْ ذُّن ُّزٍ ق َّق َّال 
س َّاءَّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ِي عَّهُّ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّس َّاءَّ إَّلْعَّن ْذُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ِي عَّهُّ مُّذ َّة َّ جَّي َّاي ِهِ وَّ لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ َّ مَّا لَّهُّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ لَّهُّ مَّال
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Abu Maryam,  from Abu Abdullah  Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}, who was asked about a man who made his bondwoman 
mudabbar (to be freed after his death). 
Imam {a.s} was asked: "Can he have relations with her if he wishes, or marry her off, or 
sell her service during his lifetime?" 
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, he may do any of these if he wishes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.121 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.8 p.263 • Al-Istibsar, Vol.4 
p.29 • Al-Wafi, Vol.10 p.628 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.23 p.119 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 49, H. 3462
